_meta.purpose: Hand-curated initial seed of published safety / responsible-scaling / governance frameworks for AI companies with a London policy presence. Slow-moving documents — most update once or twice a year.
_meta.scoring_relevance: Wired into the lobbycat scoring engine as `evidence_kind = 'safety_framework'` (v0.6 Step 11). High-signal: the company's own committed posture, not press spin.
_meta.strength_scale: 1 = aspirational/vague; 2 = principles only; 3 = named processes; 4 = named processes + commitments; 5 = enforceable thresholds with named capability tripwires and pre-deployment access.

frameworks.title: Responsible Scaling Policy
frameworks.summary: Defines ASL capability thresholds, pre-deployment evals, and required safeguards before scaling past each tier; commits to pausing if mitigations aren't ready.

frameworks.title: Usage Policy
frameworks.summary: Named prohibitions on weapons uplift, CSAM, election manipulation, and high-stakes autonomous decisions; enforcement via tier and review.

frameworks.title: Frontier Safety Framework
frameworks.summary: Critical Capability Levels (CCLs) across CBRN, cyber, ML R&D, and persuasion; commits to evals at defined model scales and to mitigations before deployment.

frameworks.title: Preparedness Framework
frameworks.summary: Tracked risk categories (biological, cyber, AI self-improvement, persuasion) with Low/Medium/High/Critical thresholds; Safety Advisory Group reviews before deployment.

frameworks.title: Model Spec
frameworks.summary: Public statement of intended model behaviour — chain-of-command, defaults, hard rules; used as a public anchor for what's a bug vs a deliberate choice.

frameworks.title: Responsible AI Standard
frameworks.summary: Six principles (fairness, reliability, privacy, inclusiveness, transparency, accountability) operationalised via internal Sensitive Uses review.

frameworks.title: Frontier AI Framework
frameworks.summary: Defines 'high-risk' vs 'critical-risk' outcomes (CBRN, cyber); commits to halt internal development if critical outcomes are reached without mitigation.

frameworks.title: Mistral Acceptable Use Policy
frameworks.summary: Named prohibited uses (CSAM, illegal content, critical-infrastructure attacks); thin on capability-threshold commitments, more posture than process.

frameworks.title: Cohere Usage Guidelines
frameworks.summary: Enterprise-oriented usage guidelines — sectoral prohibitions, customer responsibility model; no published capability-threshold framework.

frameworks.title: xAI Risk Management Framework
frameworks.summary: Draft framework outlining benchmark categories and mitigation classes; light on thresholds and external-access commitments compared to peers.

frameworks.title: Stability AI Acceptable Use Policy
frameworks.summary: Image-/audio-model focused AUP — bans CSAM, non-consensual intimate imagery, deepfake identity fraud; relies on downstream licensee enforcement.

frameworks.title: Synthesia AI Content Standards
frameworks.summary: Avatar-consent regime + content moderation tiers; explicit deepfake-of-public-figures ban and KYC for stock avatars.

frameworks.title: ElevenLabs Voice AI Safety Policy
frameworks.summary: Voice-clone consent verification, no-go list of public figures, AI Speech Classifier for detection; KYC tied to higher-risk tiers.

frameworks.title: Wayve Safety Case Approach
frameworks.summary: Safety-case methodology for autonomous driving — claims, arguments, evidence; aligned to UK Automated Vehicles Act expectations.

frameworks.title: Helsing AI Ethics Principles
frameworks.summary: Defence-AI ethics statement — democratic-values framing, meaningful human control, IHL compliance; light on independent oversight detail.

frameworks.title: Apollo Research Scheming Evaluations Methodology
frameworks.summary: Public methodology for pre-deployment scheming/deception evals; not a self-deployment policy but the spec other labs and AISI lean on.

frameworks.title: Conjecture Public Position on Frontier Limits
frameworks.summary: Public statement against training systems above defined capability lines without controllability guarantees; advocacy-flavoured, not a corporate RSP.

frameworks.title: PolyAI Enterprise AI Safety Guidelines
frameworks.summary: Voice-agent enterprise deployment guardrails — refusal taxonomies, no health-decisioning, audit logs for regulated sectors.

frameworks.title: BenevolentAI Responsible AI in Drug Discovery
frameworks.summary: Drug-discovery focused AI principles — validation requirements, dual-use review for target identification, audit trail for evidence chains.
